articles.title: SWIFT blockchain-based shared ledger progresses to MVP — 40+ banks onboarded
articles.summary: SWIFT's blockchain-based shared ledger has moved from design to MVP implementation with live transactions planned for 2026. Built on Hyperledger Besu (EVM-compatible), the ledger introduces a shared orchestration layer enabling tokenised deposit settlement and 24/7 cross-border payments.

articles.title: 50+ banks roll out new SWIFT consumer payments framework — instant settlement across 11 corridors
articles.summary: Over 50 banks worldwide have signed up to SWIFT's new payments scheme framework for retail transactions. Initial corridors cover Australia, Canada, China, Germany, India, UK and US — with guaranteed full-value delivery, upfront cost transparency, end-to-end traceability and instant settlement where available.

articles.title: Standards Release 2025: MT coexistence period ended — ISO 20022 MX now mandatory on SWIFT
articles.summary: The November 2025 Standards Release formally ended the MT/MX coexistence period for cross-border FI-to-FI payment instructions. Messages are now automatically validated against ISO 20022 rules. Cash reporting messages camt.052/053/054 are also live from SR 2025.

articles.title: Standards Release 2026: Unstructured postal addresses to be removed from SWIFT network in November
articles.summary: From November 2026, the SWIFT network will no longer accept unstructured postal addresses in CBPR+ ISO 20022 messages. All financial institutions must migrate to structured address fields (town, country) before the SR 2026 deadline to avoid payment rejections.

articles.title: 97% of SWIFT messages now sent in ISO 20022 format — adoption milestone reached
articles.summary: SWIFT confirms that 97% of messages over its network are now transmitted in ISO 20022 format, marking a historic milestone in the global adoption of the new payment messaging standard. 75% of cross-border payments reach the beneficiary institution within 10 minutes.

articles.title: CSCF v2026: Post-Quantum Security readiness added to CSP mandatory controls
articles.summary: The Customer Security Controls Framework v2026 introduces Post-Quantum Security Readiness as a new advisory control, with a roadmap toward mandatory post-quantum cryptography adoption. SWIFT CSP refresher sessions are live for all financial institutions on the SWIFT network.

articles.title: camt.110 with embedded MT 199 required for Case Management by November 2026
articles.summary: All financial institutions must be able to receive camt.110 messages with an embedded MT 199 through SWIFT Case Management by the November 2026 Standards Release. Institutions not prepared will be unable to participate in automated exception and investigation workflows.

articles.title: SWIFT AI address structuring model launched — auto-converts unstructured legacy addresses to ISO 20022
articles.summary: SWIFT has launched an AI-powered address structuring model that automatically converts legacy unstructured postal addresses into ISO 20022-compliant structured formats. The model helps financial institutions meet the November 2026 structured address deadline without manual data remediation.

articles.title: November 2027 deadline: All payment cancellations must migrate to camt.056/029 — MT no longer supported
articles.summary: SWIFT has confirmed that by November 2027, all payment cancellation messages must be in ISO 20022 formats (camt.056 and camt.029). MT-based cancellation messages will no longer be supported on the SWIFT network. Institutions should begin migration planning now.
